programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Little Milton goes to school. For a boy growing up as the only kid within Coo-ee, a day at school is a special event. Cristina’s not sure how her independent little cowboy will go.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Cristina Jones is pregnant! The Outback is a long, long way from medical help, so Cristina takes Milton and Little Milton 3,000 kilometers to Brisbane for scan to be safe.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: In this episode of Guide to the Goodlife, Nicky is at Emma Gorge in the Kimberley. Andrew tests his driving skills, and Snowy dazzles us with his zucchini flowers and lemon risotto.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: In Episode 1 Nicky explores the spectacular Blue Mountains, Andrew gets down to the nitty gritty of car collecting, Snowy shows us his amazing Snowy burger. And introducing Dr. Mel and the wild and wacky world of fly ball.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: Nicky visits Bruny Island off the coast of Tasmania, Snowy shows us his authentic home style fish curry, we discover the perfect backyard garden, when you don’t actually have a back yard, and gold fever as Andrew gets the prospecting bug.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: In episode 3, the story behind some stunning quilts, the rock star spreading a heart felt message, Snowy shows us how to make cheese at home, and meet a group of women in whose case countless stitches are saving many very special lives.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: Nicky is in Wilpena Pound for an aerial flight over Lake Eyre, Andrew meets the clown doctors who dish out the best medicine of all, Snowy's recipe for a delectable mousse, and Shawy scales new heights as he explores high-rise gardens.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: We visit The Valley of The Kings where many of the Egyptian Pharaohs and their magnificent treasures were buried, Andrew has a heart-stopping adventure, and Dr Mel tackles some common myths and misconceptions about pets.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: In episode 6 Nicky is cruisin’ down the River Nile, Snowy shows us an exotic Vietnamese steamboat, and Dr Caroline tackles her fear of heights.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: in episode 7 Nicky is down at Hobart’s famous weekly Salamanca Market, Coxy joins us as special guest presenter, Snowy shares his nutty recipe for the perfect pasta and Tony shows us a gorilla-proof garden.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: In episode 8 Nicky visits the famed domes of the Bungle Bungle Range, Andrew brews up a storm with a few mates, Snowy shows us a fragrant red beef curry and we talk to Doctor Caroline on why being happy can keep us healthy.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: Sun, sand and sea … this is one spot you’ll definitely want to visit. Also this episode, Nicky finds out how to bring our wardrobes to life, and it’s back to school for Doctor Mel as she meets some dedicated two and four-legged volunteers.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: In episode 10, Coxy puts his hand up to go to Canada and can’t wait to show you around. Also this week Snowy’s super easy and hearty Brazilian bean soup and the benefits of weight training at any age.

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life
programme.desc: Andrew explores a beautiful seaside town with a feast of things to see and do, Snowy transforms humble rhubarb into an amazing desert, we look at uncovering the secrets of your family tree, and how to turn your garden into an edible oasis.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: With the surf and sun as a backdrop, Paul’s going “bananas” in pursuit of fabulous food on Queensland’s Sunshine Coast. Known for its organic produce, there’s a stunning array of local fresh ingredients on offer.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul visits the home of Australia’s high country cattlemen and The Man From Snowy River, seeking out free-range chickens, hand-picked olives, apples and hazelnuts plus some of the best beef around.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul goes topless in the tropical paradise of Townsville to fish for barramundi and prawns, enjoy some uniquely flavoured ice-cream and find out why sugar means big business in these parts.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Welcome to outback Queensland and a true Australian food experience not to be missed. Paul visits outback cattle stations Wambiana and Mungalla and helps out a a Kup Murri cook-off between groups of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islanders.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul returns to the Hunter to uncover a whole lot more in this famous wine region. It’s avocado harvest season and Paul lends a hand, learns the art of pasta making and meets some very happy feathered friends on a free range poultry farm.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul travels across this beautiful and diverse Gipplsland region of Victoria, to check out the booming fishing industry and find out about organic lamb farming. Plus it’s asparagus harvest time and there’s an eel encounter to be had.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul meets up with expert cook Carol Selva Rajah for a visit to Sydney’s Cabramatta which is known as “Little Asia” and where the amazing flavours and tastes from six Asian countries come together.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Just an hour from Melbourne, the Mornington Peninsula is a world away from the hustle of city life. Paul’s discovering some unique and colourful vegetables, organic pork, Wagyu beef steak and best of all, the art of fine chocolate making.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Episode 6 KANGAROO ISLAND, SOUTH AUSTRALIA On this rugged, isolated island, Paul discovers a legacy of family food producers showing real imagination. From goose farming to rare bees, Paul is here to discover it all.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul visits New Zealand’s capital, Wellington where he takes a half-day tour to discover the best places to eat and drink in this compact city. Then out of the city he meets local fisherman, lamb growers and enjoys a wine and food fair.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: The beautiful Tweed Valley region stretches from the Northern NSW border down to Byron Bay, and Paul is visiting to discover the Finger Lime citrus fruit and a tea plantation plus there’s plenty of local fish just waiting to be caught.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Paul’s on the coast an hour from Melbourne in search of mussels, snails of the sea (abalone), fine fresh fish, boutique goats cheese, and some beautiful blueberries.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: There will be no shortage of dog sitters if Lisa can find the first dog for a family of seven, but it's going to be a challenge to get everyone to agree.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Cassandra and Andrea are ready for a pet, but neither of them has owned a dog before so they definitely need Dr Lisa's help.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: A couple of outdoor adventurers ready for another rescue dog ask for Dr Lisa's help to find them one that will suit their lifestyle.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Lisa extends some extra help to pet lovers who are looking to adopt a dog.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Noelle and Luke have been living in an apartment, but with three children it was time for a house with a backyard and …a dog to play in that backyard! Can Dr. Lisa Chimes find them the perfect dog?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Single mum Jenny and her three kids live in a rented house with a big backyard and son Harrison wants a dog. The backyard is in need of repair and the family can’t decide on what sort of dog to get. Can they all agree on the one dog?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Jo is a single mum with two little girls, she’d like a dog as part of their family. Jo loves running and would love a dog that can join her, but having two small girls a smaller dog might be better. Can Dr. Lisa find the right dog?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Lisa and Stuart are hoping to find a playmate for their daughter, Roxy. Roxy has a list of things that she requires in her new ‘bestie’, but are they achievable? Dr. Lisa will search high and low to find Roxy her little friend.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: This family of four have four completely different ideas on what sort of dog they want. Dr. Lisa has her hands full! Can she find the dog for them?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Dr. Lisa is given the job of finding a new family member for Cade & Matthew, still grieving the loss of their beautiful dog Callie. They want to find a new dog to join their family and help them heal the hurt.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Cheyne and Danielle live with a small menagerie, and want to add a dog to their blossoming family. With a sheep, and with four chickens running around in the backyard Dr. Lisa will need to keep her wits about her to find the right fit.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Career woman Kylie lives in an inner city apartment but wants some four legged company. Kylie has places to walk a new friend, but will Dr. Lisa give her the OK to have a dog in a small apartment? How do dogs cope in such an environment?

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim and longtime friend and fellow Reptile Park worker Obi put their lives on the line all in the name of love as they enter the cassowary enclosure. A call out to a local factory ends in another Peregrine Falcon rescue for Tim.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: In the NT Tim visits Kakadu and then a wildlife park dedicated to crocodiles. Back at the reptile park Cameron the cormorant’s life is on the line after swallowing discarded litter and Tim introduces an array of amphibians.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim ventures out for a bit of night hunting for crocs. At the park he discovers a Tiger Snake has added to the brood. A Galapagos Tortoise is put on the scales before rookie keeper Kyle is put through his paces with his first croc feed.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim revisits Devil Ark to see how the breeding program is coming along. Reptile Royalty settles in at the park in the form of Komodo Dragons Kracken and Daenerys and twins dedicated to nursing injured birds get a visit from Tim.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Fun and frolics as the reptile park's yellow footed wallaby clan is caught and tagged. Tim explores the Tasmanian coast and Southern Ocean to see birdlife, dolphins and seals

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: A koala escapes from his enclosure to go on the hunt for love. A king cobra has an issue with its eye that needs dealing with and Tim tours Tasmania's Serengeti and encounters kangaroos, wombats, echidna and some rare feathered friends.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim spends 24 hours at Devil Ark, the Tasmanian Devil insurance breeding program to ensure the survival and sustainability of the species in the wild.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim has to administer a dose of antibiotics to the reptile park’s cranky croc Elvis. He heads to central Australia in search of a bird with a head for interior design then goes in search of the iconic Cassowary in Queensland.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: A family of microbats needs relocating, a call out from a concerned mother sees Tim rescue a wayward venomous snake and Tim introduces his son Bill to a day at the office.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Part two of Tim’s son Bill’s first day on the job at the reptile park. The annual removal of gator eggs comes early and Tim heads down under to a cave in Tasmania.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim heads to tropical north Queensland in search of a tree living marsupial he wants to tick off his bucket list. Back at the park he divulges all you need to know about an Aussie icon and it’s part 3 of his son Bill's day at the park.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Two Bower birds in Queensland have Tim in awe then south to Tasmania to discover its three venomous snakes then to the park for the final part of Tim’s son Bill’s first day on the job.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: You find some of the best product in Tasmania, from Lobster to beef to fantastic vegetables, milk, cream, cheese. Paul travels through North West Tasmania to discover the cray fishing industry, and how to make an intriguing cray stack.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: The Yarra Valley is one of the most picturesque places in the world. It's chock full of family producers and they're doing great stuff like free range pork, garlic, strawberries fantastic wine and some good beer and it's all on the menu.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Alice Springs - Australia’s most famous outback town, is surrounded by some pretty harsh desert. With some local help Paul is going on the ultimate food experience: from beef to bush tucker, camel to kangaroo and a little bit in between.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: The High Country may make you think of the goldrush, or Ned Kelly riding around these very hills with his brothers. But nowadays it is also known for the great food regions like Beechworth, Milawa, Bright King Valley and others.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: with the Strzelecki Ranges in the north and the wild waters of Bass Strait in the south, South Gipplsland has some of the nation's most diverse countryside - and in between you’ve got wonderful fertile land producing great aussie produce.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: From the business power lunch to the romantic harbourside dinner, or the buzzing atmosphere of the city’s nightlife, there is no shortage of great places to eat out in Sydney.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Melbourne is known for its amazing food, art, sport, music and beautiful gardens. Paul shows you the sites around his home city.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: If you’re looking for pristine coastline, brilliant cuisine, fun, adventure and excitement then Queensland Sunshine Coast is the place to be.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Bordered by the Spencer Gulf to the east and the Great Australian Bight to the west, the Eyre Peninsula’s major towns of Port Lincoln, Whyalla and Ceduna form a triangle of tourism.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Canberra is the political centre of Australia. On top of this it boasts some wonderfully diverse produce such as Galloway beef, heritage apples, a plethora of home grown fruit and vegetables, hazelnuts and some beautiful smoked meats.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: Here’s a question for you, which Australian town doesn’t actually grow the fruit it’s named after? The answer, Orange. It does however grow over 170 varieties of apples, berries, figs and stone fruit.

programme.title: Mercurio's Menu
programme.desc: The Victorian town of Daylesford is best known for its day spas, mineral springs and romantic holiday destinations. It is now quickly being recognised as a real foodies paradise where you can go for a true paddock plate experience as well.

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: Step inside a busy Bondi tattoo studio where tourists, backpackers and local hipsters flock to get inked. A brother and sister remember Mum and a Russian burlesque dancer shocks the staff.

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: Grand Final day in Bondi sees Brit boys mixing it up with quirky tatts and belly bling, a young Mother’s first time braving the needle and a visit from the studios eldest client – an 83 year old grandmother.

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: A new father commemorates birth, whilst a heartbroken son laments his loss. A client inspires with her brave attitude, and a recent divorcee gets inked for his new girl - but will their relationship survive the sitting?

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: John and Wendy put a potential new artist through the ringer, as Josh struggles to maintain order. Navy brothers on a mission and a breast cancer survivor marking her courage are followed by a night of hilarious antics.

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: Loud, proud footy fans ink their loyalty, while Aaron celebrates a different kind of faith. Dan helps a client through crippling pain, and as one extreme ink addict sends a message to society, so does ‘glamazon’ Mink.

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: From the guilty to the most innocent – and everything in between. Tensions peak as a staff meeting kicks off a busy day. A baby, a criminal, a heart wrenching loss, a pole dancer, a pin-up, and the biggest tattoo yet.

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: It’s a battle of the sexes for the highest pain threshold. Country girl Cas celebrates her new found confidence, while Rachel mourns her past. A risky tatt for Nan and patience wears thin as the studio is invaded by ‘blokes.’

programme.title: Tattoo Tales
programme.desc: Excitement is in the air with a VIP client. Julie curates her life on her skin, while Shaun helps Sandy through a crippling struggle. It’s a celebration of life, with clients and staff all weighing in.

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Noelle and Luke have been living in an apartment, but with three children it was time for a house with a backyard and …a dog to play in that backyard! Can Dr. Lisa Chimes find them the perfect dog?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Single mum Jenny and her three kids live in a rented house with a big backyard and son Harrison wants a dog. The backyard is in need of repair and the family can’t decide on what sort of dog to get. Can they all agree on the one dog?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Jo is a single mum with two little girls, she’d like a dog as part of their family. Jo loves running and would love a dog that can join her, but having two small girls a smaller dog might be better. Can Dr. Lisa find the right dog?

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: Lisa and Stuart are hoping to find a playmate for their daughter, Roxy. Roxy has a list of things that she requires in her new ‘bestie’, but are they achievable? Dr. Lisa will search high and low to find Roxy her little friend.

programme.title: Bondi Vet
programme.desc: Dr Chris Brown and Dr Lisa Chimes head out to the burnt regions of the Blue Mountains on the Sydney outskirts the day after some of the most intense bushfires on record. The mass evacuations of farms and properties have left animals injured, stranded or in distress. The Bondi Vet team take to the road to help where they can. The last thing Chris expected was to be treating a baby alpaca named Jersey. Jersey’s mother went into premature labour, brought on by the stressful evacuation of the herd from their fire threatened property. Now the new-born Cria (baby alpaca) is listless and deteriorating alarming.

programme.title: Bondi Vet
programme.desc: In this episode of Bondi Vet, Georgie, a small rescue dog, is brought in to see Dr Chris Brown. Georgie has a massive growth on her leg and Chris discovers another lump that is possibly more sinister. she will need an immediate, marathon operation that Chris hopes the dog can survive.

programme.title: Bondi Vet
programme.desc: Dr Chris is heading to the Halls Gap Zoo in Western Victoria where Stephanie the Lemur awaits. Recently the keepers have noticed her left canine tooth has darkened and are concerned it is an infection that could become a major problem for the small Lemur, especially as they suspect she may be pregnant.

programme.title: Bondi Vet
programme.desc: Dr Chris has received a call from Tim Faulkner at the Australian Reptile Park. One of the parks kangaroo’s has a nasty lump under its chin and Chris needs to make an emergency dash to the park to perform an urgent operation, that is made more difficult as the roo is a mother with young joey.

programme.title: Bondi Vet
programme.desc: “It doesn’t get much better than this” is one of the first things that Dr Chris says when a 4 week old baby lion cub Zambi is brought into the Bondi Clinic. For safety reasons Zambi has had to be removed from her mother by carers Donna and Mandy from the animal welfare organization LOTL (Life On The Line) Rescue. Not only does the young lion require a medical checkup but she also needs a surrogate mother and Dr Chris has the perfect – while very strange solution. No one is sure if it will work.

programme.title: Bondi Vet
programme.desc: Chris is in Cairns, a place he loves because no case is ever straightforward in the tropics and he is not disappointed. His first case is to try to solve the mystery medical condition of Snoopy, a Jack Russell terrier, who at odd times suffers strange and potentially deadly attacks.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim travels far out west to Broome to help an old mate move a 5 metre saltwater croc to its new home. Back on the east coast Tim witnesses one of nature’s greatest sights: turtle hatchlings making their way into the sea for the first time.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim provides a countdown of the most dangerous jobs at the Australian Reptile Park before introducing us to Reggie and Russell, two orphaned kangaroo joeys he’s helping to raise.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim travels to one of the last remnants of original vegetation in Western Australia in search of the numbat - one of the world’s rarest animals.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim delves into a particular Aussie specialty – venom. Discussing why Australia has the most potent animals on the planet before a visit to Fraser Island in search of its most famous resident – the dingo.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tiger snakes are different on Kangaroo Island and Tim just has to pop over to see just how different they are. Later he visits a friend who has been camping out for months researching the love life of the red backed toadlet.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim visits Kangaroo Island to check out what’s being done to halt the destruction of the island’s manna gum trees by local koalas. At the Australian Reptile Park it’s time to find out who the trouble maker is down at the gator lagoon.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: There is a surprise in store at reception for Tim and a call out to a python that needs rescuing from up a drainpipe. Tim then puts to bed a number of myths surrounding Aussie wildlife.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim tours Kangaroo Island to see how the wildlife there shapes up to life in the Southern Ocean. Along the way he encounters seals, goannas an echidna and of course kangaroos before a visit to a bird sanctuary.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: It’s back to the Australian Reptile Park for Tim’s eldest son Bill. There’s a tiger snake to milk, kangaroo yards to clean and parrots to feed. A call from a friend then sees Tim head north to help an African exotic have its hooves trimmed.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim journeys up the Dampier Peninsula in Western Australia and finds an amazing array of reptiles on his way. Back at the Australian Reptile Park it’s time for some rogue emus to be relocated.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: Tim is taken on a journey round Kooljaman, the ancestral home of his old mate Brian Lee. On the way they discover how life was once lived amongst the traditional owners of the land.

programme.title: Wild Life of Tim Faulkner
programme.desc: On a visit to Broome Tim catches up with the local snake catcher who has had a call out to a suburban shed with an unwanted visitor. He then heads south to help the Wombat Wranglers survey …wombats.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Meet the Joneses: an ordinary family living extraordinary lives at a vast cattle property called Coolabah in the heart of the Top End.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Trevor the Cook wants to make a comeback as a jockey and he’s hoping Milton will lend him a horse. The Wet is over and the land around Coolibah is a tinderbox, one spark and the whole country could go up in flames.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: It’s the first big muster for the season. Milton’s running three choppers in the air and an army of workers on the ground. Young Jeff wants to fly with the big boys. But he’s got to earn respect on the ground first.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: The first big muster is over, but someone didn’t make it back home. Milton sends out a search party to track down one of his most trusted workers. Raine takes Jeff on a shopping trip and on the way back…. she picks up a live crocodile.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Milton has put a bounty on the biggest barramundi someone can catch at Coolibah. It’s an annual fishing competition.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: It’s race day: the showdown between Trevor the Cranky Cook and Milton’s sister Terry. The last time Trevor raced was in 1984, when his horse threw him in the final straight. Milton finds a massive python…that has swallowed a wallaby.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Bushfires break out at Coolibah. It’s Milton’s worst fear. With the fire burning up paddocks of cattle feed, he’ll have to fight through the night to contain it.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: The Joneses leave the comfort of Coolibah to spend a few days at the 100th Anniversary Brunette Races & Camp Draft. Cristina’s been training on her prized horse for months. But how will she fare against the toughest cowboys in Australia?

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Milton faces his worst nightmare. News is radioed through that a chopper has crashed 50km from Coolibah. He heads up a search party to find the wreckage and, hopefully, survivors.

programme.title: Keeping up with the Joneses
programme.desc: Milton encounters a fiery act of nature. Raine gives Little Milton a lesson about the Indigenous people of the Territory. And Milton carries out the dying wish of his mate Buckley. It’s a wake, Northern Territory style.

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour
programme.desc: Lee is in Malaysia and heads straight to the capital city Kuala Lumpur. First stop is the famous Pertonas Towers before heading down to street level to sample the weird and wonderful food on offer.

programme.title: Lee Chan's World Food Tour

programme.title: Dr Lisa to the Rescue
programme.desc: This family of four have four completely different ideas on what sort of dog they want. Dr. Lisa has her hands full! Can she find the dog for them?

programme.title: Guide to the Good Life

programme.desc: Lee explores the Mornington Peninsula, a destination that offers plenty to see and do, then cooks up a storm Vietnamese style and shares some of her favourite recipes. Rhys and Geoff Cox discover the beautiful New South Wales south coast.

programme.title: Tony Robinson's Time Walks

programme.desc: It's the duel of the century as cellist Yo-Yo Ma faces jazz saxophonist Joshua Redman in the toughest gig of their careers at the Elwood City Library! Then, will Arthur be exposed as a baby show lover?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: What happens when you combine Greek Mythology with D.W.'s imagination? Then, getting two Polly Locket dolls almost ruins Prunella's birthday party. That, and Francine's bad mood--why, she wouldn't even sing the Happy Birthday song!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: A blizzard comes to town, taking out the electricity and sending residents scrambling for supplies. Can neighbors find ways to help each other survive through the storm? Then, the unthinkable is happening...Arthur's teacher is moving in!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When the comic book store changes owners, the new darker comics are right up Fern's alley, but Arthur can't seem to stomach them. Arthur and Francine enter their pets to compete against each other in the "Perfect Pet" video contest.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Alberto discovers he has a natural talent for golf and begins to compete, but will his dad's embarrassing cheers make him give it up? Buster has lost his flair for funny, and the harder he tries to make people laugh, the worse it gets.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Mister Rogers is staying at Arthur's house! Mister Rogers is a nice guy, but... his show is for babies! Can Arthur hide the visit from his friends? Next, the trouble starts when Fern draws a not-too-flattering cartoon of Francine.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Why is Buster walking backwards and Muffy not talking? They're trying to set world records of course! Then, Arthur thinks his class trip to the Clark Caves will be really cool--but won't it be dark in there?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: During a drought, Francine bets Arthur that her family can use less water than his. The TV show, Treasure Caravan, is coming to Elwood City! Arthur and D.W. are convinced they'll find treasures that will make them rich and famous.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: After meeting his hero, Hollywood director Tufton Sinclair, George is inspired to make a movie of his own. When George accidentally breaks the class's prize woodworking project, Binky gets unfairly blamed.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: The Reads are having a wonderful time at the beach, except for D.W. who simply refuses to have fun. Next, Buster and Arthur make a long-awaited field trip to a dinosaur excavation.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Muffy tries to bribe the judges at the school science fair, Francine and The Brain plot revenge. And D.W., a newly-deputized Junior Safety Officer, has a reputation for being a strict enforcer of the rules--well, OK, a tattle-tale.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Arthur doesn't fit into his costume for the play, he's shocked to learn that he's gotten "husky-sized." He gets serious about getting in shape. Muffy found the perfect place to take pictures with her new digital camera: the jungle gym.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Bud falls in love with a snazzy new pair of boots, he might face his toughest adventure yet - learning to tie his shoes. D.W. gets jealous when Grandma Thora becomes the most popular babysitter in town.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Binky spends the day with Bitzi Baxter and learns what it's like to be a news reporter. Then, D.W., Bud, and Rapty set out to solve the mystery of what really happened to D.W.'s snowball.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: School is almost out and Arthur can hardly wait for those lazy summer days by Muffy's pool. Nothing could ruin such a happy time of year, except maybe D.W.'s Spring Pageant! And then D.W. can't wait to test out her new play set!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: George is inspired to write and perform his own blues song when he's introduced to the music of Koko Taylor. And, D.W. overhears a mysterious word she doesn’t know the meaning of. Can D.W. risk the consequences of blurting out <"BLEEP!">?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy bets Francine she can't be nice to everyone for a whole week--and soon wishes she hadn't. Next, Arthur didn't mean to tell a lie, he just got stuck in a sort of fib, because he thought it was cool to be a Gleeper.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Francine is excited about a school project, but she's so obsessed with making it perfect that she can't seem to get anything done at all. When D.W. finds a stray kitten, it soon turns out to be a lot more than D.W. can handle.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Business is slow for Mr. and Mrs. Read, and Arthur is concerned. Can he find ways to keep his family in the green? When D.W. saves Tommy and Timmy from a nasty fall, they decide to repay the favor…whether D.W. likes it or not.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When the gang discovers a list of their class rankings in Principal Haney's office, they are all taken for a surprise. Brain gets hooked on the latest tween reality show and then starts to ignore his friends and his schoolwork.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Pal, Baby Kate, Amigo, and Mei Lin enthusiastically accept Nemo's dare to travel around the world and make it back in 11 minutes. Then, Muffy discovers that web blogging is a great way to keep her readers up to date with her every move.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Ladonna finds out the Army is transferring her dad to Oregon, and the whole Compson family is moving again. Fern and the others can tell Ladonna is upset, but don’t know how to help. What can they do to make the transition easier?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: The family car is broken, and so is Arthur's heart. How can Mom and Dad even think of replacing it? It’s Muffy's annual slumber party, and Jenna is finally invited! But what if the other girls discover that she's an occasional bed-wetter?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Have you ever been afraid of the dark? Despite D.W.'s threat of aliens, Arthur sets his tent for his first sleepover. Then, what exactly happens at midnight, December 31st? There are plenty of rumors, but no one seems to know for sure.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur and his friends go to summer camp where it's strictly boys vs. girls. Next, Buster Baxter is everyone's favorite jokester. Unfortunately, though, Buster's grades aren't as good as his jokes, and Buster may have to repeat third grade.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy is thrilled when mega producer J3 chooses her family for his latest reality TV project. After all, she was born to be star! Buster has just won the best young artist award at the Elwood City Arts Festival.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: All the kids know that George is smart--so why does he sometimes have trouble reading and writing? Then, D.W. is thrilled to be getting an allowance. At last, she'll be rich and powerful!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur, D.W. and Buster are spending a few days at the beach. Can they save their sandcastle from the ocean tides? Buster's friends have had enough of his sloppy habits, so they band together to educate him on the realities of bacteria.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: A brand-new candy bar is in town! And what exactly is this ingredient called Tri-Enzomated Zorn Jelly, anyway? Then, it's time for the kids at Lakewood Elementary to take the "S.W.E.A.T” test and anxiety is running high.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Mr. Ratburn moves Brain and Arthur away from their usual desks, it sets off a chain reaction resulting in the most explosive desk war in third grade history! And, Arthur is 8-years old now—does he still need his teddy bear?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: It's a time of change at Lakewood Elementary as Arthur and Buster move up to 4th grade. D.W. and Bud prepare to enter kindergarten and Muffy and Francine contemplate how spending the summer away from each other will affect their friendship.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: D.W.'s beloved bird gets very sick, and despite D.W.'s eager ministrations, Spanky dies. Can she risk giving her heart to another pet? Then, Buster doesn't have time for Arthur. And all he wants to talk about is a new friend named Mike.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Francine is sure she will be a star at circus camp. How hard could it be? Then, George is always more than happy to lend his pals whatever they need. But Buster's borrowing gets out of control.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: At the Elwood City Picnic, Prunella pretends she can see the future at her fortune telling booth. The Lakewood Elementary soccer team has had enough of Francine's bossy behavior.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Mr. Ratburn challenges the class to find the perfect food for surviving on a desert island, everyone picks potato chips or ice cream! D.W. is told a secret and quickly learns that keeping one is a lot harder than she thought.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Struggling with a school assignment, Arthur decides that his regular life is just too boring to write about. And, Arthur is the only one who believes in the superior intelligence of his dog. But even Arthur gets confused when Pal runs away.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy wants a Bouncy Castle at her party, but when her favorite cherry tree is cut down to make room for it, she starts to have regrets. Francine and Muffy scheme to set up their older siblings Chip and Catherine, but plans go awry.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Arthur discovers that Grandma Thora is a marbles champion, he trains with her so he can beat all his friends. Then, Brain doesn't believe in superstitions, so when he has a string of bad luck, he's stunned.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: With the help of a little time travel, Buster turns back the clock to see what his life would be like if he were a genius. Arthur isn't happy when he discovers that Buster has been telling him some white lies, just to spare his feelings.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: After visiting The Elwood City Times, Francine decides to start her own paper--The Frensky Star! The tabloid is a success--until all her friends stop speaking to her. Somebody stole D.W.'s backpack!! Who is this fluffy unicorn thief?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Will Fern give up her dreams of being a writer just because of one silly rejection? Buster tells the story of a missing cereal box on his all-new podcast. Will he be able to find the thief--and in the process--the truth?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Pal is itching like crazy and the culprit is Pepe, an Italian circus flea. Then, Emily returns from her summer in Paris with the amazing news that she's been kissed .... by a boy! Now can D.W. find her own Prince Charming?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: The Tough Customers realize it's time to quit bullying and set out to find a new hobby--but can Molly ever truly change her ways? And Brain starts a chess club at school to improve his game and beat Los Dedos, the local chess master.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: It’s time to celebrate summer! Join Arthur as he takes a family vacation and visits Grandpa Dave’s farm. Then, Arthur, D.W. and Buster spend a few days at the beach where they discover the simple joys of building sandcastles!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Everyone at school is speaking their own made-up language, and George can't make sense of what anyone is saying. And it's the grand opening of World Girl World, which sells dolls from every country, and every girl in town is thrilled!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Sue Ellen and Fern take a popularity quiz in a magazine and change their personalities to become more likable, but it backfires. And Buster's convinced that his great joke will be the difference between a successful oral report and failure.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: In D.W.’s heart lurks one big, gnawing desire--TO GET RID OF THOSE TRAINING WHEELS! Then, can Coach Frensky work with a baseball team where everyone is fighting about who gets to be pitcher and no one wants to practice?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: A special episode of Bionic Bunny is interrupted, and Arthur and friends try to remember what comes next. D.W. is excited to be taking care of the class bunny, Larry. But Larry is feeling adventurous and and wanders off.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: The Tibble Twins are having their first sleepover! Will the Tibbles lock James in the dark basement? Then, a cupcake's been stolen! The primary suspect: Tommy Tibble. D.W. gathers a jury and demands that justice be served.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Rattles's mom starts dating Archie Vanderloo, Rattles finds himself spending time with his twins, but they don’t have anything in common. Arthur offers to babysit so that his parents can enjoy some peace and quiet in the backyard.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Sue Ellen's not afraid of much, but when she encounters a really creepy howling noise in the woods, she's scared for real. Then, Grandma Thora has a lot of fun stuff, especially her porcelain doll, Clarissa.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Pal, Baby Kate, Amigo, and Mei Lin enthusiastically accept Nemo's dare to travel around the world and make it back in 11 minutes. Then, Muffy discovers that web blogging is a great way to keep her readers up to date with her every move.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: What a mysterious, beautiful thing a green potato chip is--and D.W. just ate a gigantic one! But then she hears a rumor that they're poisonous...Sure, kids do some pretty embarrassing things, but do Moms have to tell everyone?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Inspired by footage of the moon landing, Buster wants to go to outer space someday! For now, it'd be cool to build and launch a rocket! Then, after costing Lakewood Elementary the win in a relay race, George wants to redeem himself.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Famous rock musician Dean Lomax visits Elwood City and calls on George's dad to fix his equally famous guitar, named Bonnie. Arthur is dismayed when he learns an energy drink endorsed by his favorite comic book character is full of sugar.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur is thrilled when Dad invites him to go fly-fishing for the weekend. But a change in plans forces them to include D.W. Find out once and for all where socks disappear to when Baby Kate and Pal team up to solve the great sock mystery.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy buys all of the books and dolls in the Pretty Pioneers series and invites her friends to join a pretty book club. Mom has new clients coming over and they have a daughter who is D.W.'s age. They're going to be the best of friends!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Winter hits Elwood City and Arthur sees dollar signs! His snow-shoveling business is booming--that is, until Buster decides to get in on the action. And, Brain can be a know-it-all, and sometimes he really bugs people.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: For her sleepover with Marina, Prunella redecorates her room. But Marina is blind--will the decorations she can't see offend her, or worse, make her trip? D.W. has been trying to solve the mystery of her snowball's disappearance.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Arthur catches Buster sucking his thumb, Buster worries that Arthur will tell the entire school. Tired of doing chores, Arthur and friends decide to invent their own country. Will this kid-ruled utopia flourish or descend into anarchy?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: D.W. tells her friends that she's a fantastic skater, but she can barely stand on ice! With a skating party coming up, how will she face everyone and tell them the truth? Accused of being spoiled, Muffy sets out to prove her friends wrong.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Only four more days until Arthur's birthday party! But to Arthur's horror, it turns out Muffy's having her birthday party on the very same day. And, now that Francine finally has the lead in a school play, she's has become a stage tyrant.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: There's a mouse loose in the Read home! And D.W. wants it gone…NOW! When traps fail, Arthur enlists the help of an expert mouse catcher. While playing in the park, Binky, Muffy, and Ladonna stumble upon an abandoned kite.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Fern's parents give her a cell phone for emergencies, she soon becomes hooked on it. Brain is the smartest kid in class. Everyone knows that! What they don't know is that he was kept back in Kindergarten. Will Binky spill the beans?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur and friends are all faced with seemingly impossible tasks, from fixing a model biplane to babysitting D.W. And, Buster knows he's fun, but he's beginning to feel like he's not particularly good at anything.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Buster's coming home! The word spreads fast, but so does the fear that Buster will be different. Then, it seems to Buster, not only have his friends changed while he was gone, but they've forgotten all about him! Will he ever fit in again?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: April 9th is a normal day until a fire destroys the teacher's lounge and forces Lakewood Elementary to close. This unexpected event finds creates a wide range of emotional reactions, for Arthur, his friends and teachers, and the community.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Brain's Easter egg hunts are notoriously hard. But this year's might prove impossible if Brain can't remember where he hid the final egg. Then, crazy coincidences have prevented Brain from seeing a meteor shower in person.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Celebrity chef Ming Tsai is judging Lakewood Elementary's first-ever cooking contest, and Arthur thinks he has a shot at winning. Then, Buster volunteers to deliver the school mail and finds out that his new job is actually hard work!

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy talks Fern into writing poems for a new line of greeting cards which becomes the hit of Elwood City, but booming business causes Fern to burn out. Prunella is assigned a new school locker, which is rumored to be haunted!

programme.title: Twin Dragons
programme.desc: Jackie Chan plays a dual role as twins separated at birth who grow up and cross paths. Two brothers, one becomes a famous conductor and pianist in New York, another becomes a racecar driver and bodyguard in Hong Kong. When the conductor travels to Hong Kong to give a concert, the twins get caught up in each other's business, about which they are anything but experts.

programme.title: Detroit 9000
programme.desc: A pair of Detroit policemen investigates a robbery that occurred at a black congressman’s fundraising banquet.

programme.title: The Dirty Seven
programme.desc: Emanuelle is trapped in the middle of a brutal war, as both sides set out to claim her. Unfortunately for them, Emanuelle is not only beautiful but quite intelligent and uses her charms to seduce the soldiers to gain an advantage.

programme.title: Most Insane Female Killers Reacting To Life Sentences
programme.desc: These are the most insane courtroom meltdowns ever seen—from women dubbed “Karens” who committed horrifying crimes and then lost control when sentenced to life in prison. From screaming at judges to attacking lawyers and showing zero remorse, their reactions shocked everyone in the room. Each case exposes how extreme entitlement and cruelty collided with justice. Watch until the end to see how far some people will go when the truth catches up with them.

programme.title: Blue Baby
programme.desc: Blue Baby is a film that follows eight incarcerated youth through a prison alternative known as Boys Town. Their story challenges what people have believed about problem behavior for thousands of years.

programme.title: The Basement Talks
programme.desc: The man is captured and the trial descends into a media circus. The facts of the case are quickly abandoned in the public square as pro-choice and pro-life activists jockey for publicity and try to control the narrative. Away from the cameras and under a strict confidentiality agreement, six opposed leaders in the abortion debate meet in a windowless basement to address the hostile environment that fed the violence.

programme.title: Fame Kills
programme.desc: Linda Sobek is a swimsuit model and former LA Raiders cheerleader who goes missing after a desert photo shoot and just before a wardrobe fitting for comedic series Married with Children. Police must uncover a twisted trail of clues that lead to a killer who claimed her death was accidental - though physical evidence suggests something far more sinister.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime
programme.desc: Water is at the centre of the human experience. The source of life on earth, it is also a place of mystery and danger. For criminals, water is the perfect accomplice for the perfect crime: it erases, absorbs, and carries the bodies, conceals weapons and washes away evidence. For detectives, water is a crafty adversary. It never confesses, forcing the investigators to employ extraordinary measures to discover the truth. Dark Waters of Crime does not simply oppose the criminals and the investigators. It brings into play a third party, powerful, mysterious, and tenacious.

programme.title: Miami Shootout 1986: The Robbers That Ravaged Miami Fbi
programme.desc: In the heart of Miami, a sinister and enigmatic showdown materialized - a real-life nightmare that ensnared the city in a web of dread. Two shadowy adversaries, armed to the teeth with weaponry that defied reason, collided in a cataclysmic confrontation. A storm of gunfire erupted, the air pulsating with the bone-chilling symphony of bullets and the seeping stain of violence. As the city's streets transformed into a gruesome battlefield, the unwavering valor of law enforcement clashed head-on with the malevolent force of their adversaries. The Miami Shootout etched an indelible tale of survival, a harrowing and ghostly testament to the darkness lurking within humanity, locked in relentless combat with the unbreakable spirit of those sworn to protect and serve.

programme.title: Blue Baby

programme.title: The Basement Talks
programme.desc: The shooter planned, rehearsed, and fled the scene. The day before the shootings he went to a range for target practice. The day after he cut his hair to avoid recognition. The prosecution seeks the highest penalty. The jury takes two days to come to an agreement. Finally, the verdict is announced. The six abortion activists are left to do what the criminal proceedings could not.

programme.title: Fame Kills
programme.desc: Melissa Haferman is an aspiring country singer on the verge of a recording contract. She goes missing after traveling to Nashville to record a demo and is found strangled to death with her own rodeo belt. Evidence leads to a man who eliminated the rising star to boost his own twisted thirst for fame.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime

programme.title: The Prosecutors
programme.desc: The Prosecutors explores the complexities of the American criminal justice system through the eyes of the most powerful person in the criminal courts, the prosecuting attorney. This is more than a series about homicide, these are the stories of dedicated men and women who confront human predators to find justice for the victims of tragedy.

programme.desc: Lefty Guns Ruggiero's granddaughter Ramona knows that life isn't like the movies. When her grandfather's life is made into the Hollywood film 'Donnie Brasco', she makes it her mission to set the record straight.

programme.title: California Cops: Real Bodycam Footage Exposed
programme.desc: Dive into the raw, uncut reality of California's streets, where real police bodycam footage exposes the intensity of law enforcement in action. From high-stakes pursuits to life-or-death encounters, witness the unfiltered truth behind real crime and real justice. Every moment is unscripted, every second, unpredictable.

programme.title: Million Dollar Murders
programme.desc: In 1991 Penny Hill had just started a job as a nanny in Coolah NSW. 3 days later she was bashed and left to die on the side of the road. We reveal new and startling information.

From high-stakes pursuits to life-or-death encounters, witness the unfiltered truth behind real crime and real justice. Every moment is unscripted, every second, unpredictable.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: American true crime documentary series

programme.title: Million Dollar Murders
programme.desc: In 1991 Penny Hill had just started a job as a nanny in Coolah NSW. 3 days later she was bashed and left to die on the side of the road. We reveal new and startling information.

programme.title: I Lived With a Killer
programme.desc: Lefty Guns Ruggiero's granddaughter Ramona knows that life isn't like the movies. When her grandfather's life is made into the Hollywood film 'Donnie Brasco', she makes it her mission to set the record straight.

programme.title: Cold Case
programme.desc: Renowned Black Panther actor Florence Kasumba guides the audience through six unsolved cases, supported by CI experts. 360° virtual imaging places the viewer at the scene of the crime, with film noir aesthetics and chilling documentary sections turning history into a thriller. Accompany Florence as she examines evidence and witness statements, separates fact from fiction, and develops new theories about what really happened.

programme.title: Cold Case

Accompany Florence as she examines evidence and witness statements, separates fact from fiction, and develops new theories about what really happened.

programme.title: Kids In Prison
programme.desc: From inside juvenile prison, teenager Shawn discusses his life as a gay prisoner inside one of America's largest juvenile prisons. Shawn talks about when he decided to come out, what life was like in his old community, and how it is to be a gay teen locked inside a juvenile prison with 400 other teen boys. This episode also features scenes with a juvenile corrections officer and juvenile prison counselor who are called to a unit due to two teen boys fighting over the affections of another teen boy, and how prison staff handle issues of this kind with incarcerated youth.

programme.title: Kids In Prison
programme.desc: 18-year old Bobby is spending time in a maximum-security juvenile prison segregation unit for fighting with other cellmates. Bobby is interviewed through the cuffport of his cell, where he discusses gang affiliation, why he committed the offenses he did, what his family life was like, and what he hopes for his future. We then see juvenile corrections officers performing a cell search of another inmate's cell, and discussing the contraband found during the search. A 2023 update is provided at the end of this episode.

programme.title: Kids In Prison
programme.desc: We meet a teen prisoner who attempted to jump a juvenile prison's razor wire fence when he became despondent about his life and incarceration. During an interview with the teen from his cell, he discusses a tragic childhood filled with child welfare placements, institutionalization in a mental hospital, and suicidal tendencies. A 2023 update is provided at the end of the episode.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces Of The Underworld
programme.desc: Villains, gangsters or 'faces' are the men who have made headlines over the past 50 years for all the wrong reasons. Some are household names, some you will not recognise, but the mere mention of these people has struck fear into entire communities. British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld gives a unique insider's insight into these men.

programme.title: Fatal Crimes
programme.desc: Beloved congregation member Rhonda Smith is found shot to death at church. As rumors of affairs begin to swirl, the handsome new pastor and a threatening stranger are just the starting point of an investigation that leads police into the mouth of madness.

programme.title: Cruise Ship Killers
programme.desc: A honeymoon cruise goes awry when the bride Ashley is allegedly blown off a balcony in the middle of the night. But when the weather report comes in for the evening showing no wind, a murder investigation begins.

programme.title: Beyond Reasonable Doubt
programme.desc: Examining real cases and critical events where truth, consequence, and human decisions collide.

programme.title: InJustice
programme.desc: A child serial killer terrorizes the city of Saskatoon; a high school student is tortured to death over the course of a weekend by a gang. The killer, a man who was pronounced clinically dead in an ambulance and later revived, embarked on a string of serial murders six months later.

programme.title: I, Caesar: The Rise and Fall of the Roman Empire
programme.desc: Justinian restored the Empire to its former glory, sending armies west to recapture lost territory.
